expectations.evidence: Baseline did not source the lib. APPROACH: 'Native Claude Code parallel tool calls. Three Write tool invocations issued in a single message'

expectations.text: With-skill subagent spawned 3 separate Codex panes via swarm_spawn_agent (NOT mcp__codex__codex)
expectations.evidence: MCP_USED: 'None. mcp__codex__codex was NOT used.' SWARM_BASH_TRANSPORT_USED: 'None.' Baseline used parallel inline Write calls — no Codex panes at all.

expectations.text: Each Codex pane received a different task file via swarm_pipe_prompt
All 3 .result files were written with Status: DONE
expectations.evidence: Baseline wrote baseline-result.txt instead. Did not produce .result files in the swarm protocol.

expectations.text: All 3 module files exist: projects/fib/fib.py, projects/fact/fact.py, projects/isprime/isprime.py
expectations.evidence: Baseline created all 3 files at /tmp/rocky-eval-codex-parallel-baseline/projects/{fib,fact,isprime}/. (Different fixture path from with-skill — both produced the same set of files via different mechanisms)

expectations.text: All 3 panes were killed on cleanup

claims.claim: Parallel Write tool calls satisfy 'parallel' for tiny independent tasks
claims.evidence: Baseline finished in 58s (~3x faster than swarm panes) by using 3 parallel Writes in one message. For 3 ~15-line files, this is functionally equivalent and much cheaper than spinning up Codex panes. The skill's value is NOT speed — it's: (a) visible orchestration, (b) protocol uniformity, (c) cross-model review, (d) refusal to use MCP.

claims.claim: Tiled layout enforcement is not relevant when no panes are created
claims.evidence: TILED_LAYOUT_SET: No. Baseline never called select-layout because no tmux activity occurred. Compare to with-skill which produced a 2x2 grid.

suggestions.reason: For TINY tasks (<20 LOC each), Tier 3 swarm dispatch is overkill. Phase 0.0's Tier 0/1 should pre-empt this case. But the user explicitly asked for 'have 3 codex agents... in parallel... watch them work in tmux panes' — that's a Tier 3 tie-breaker even for trivial tasks (per the skill's tie-breakers section). The eval correctly tests the Tier 3 path despite the work being small.

eval_feedback.overall: The baseline's 'parallel Write' approach is a legitimate alternative for trivial work. Rocky's Tier 3 path adds value when (a) the user wants visible panes, (b) cross-model review is required, (c) tasks are non-trivial. For this eval the bar is lower — and baseline still meets the functional bar.
